title: thuja occidentalis f. rosenthalii
intro: Thuja occidentalis f. rosenthalii, also known as Western Red Cedar, is a cultivar of the Eastern White Cedar tree native to North America, distinguished by its distinctive foliage and compact growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as colds, coughs, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Thuja occidentalis f. rosenthalii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be applied topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
